methodsAttempted.query: The University of Tennessee, Knoxville QS row 510
methodsAttempted.notes: Confirmed target metadata from QS 2026 local ranking row: rank =607, Knoxville, United States.

methodsAttempted.query: site:utk.edu OR site:tennessee.edu University of Tennessee Knoxville generative AI artificial intelligence teaching policy academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl web search returned UT System policy, UTK writing center guidance, OIT news, provost AI page, UT Libraries guide, and College of Law guide.

methodsAttempted.query: UTK AI syllabus statements GenAI writing students
methodsAttempted.notes: Found UTK Writing Center student-facing GenAI writing guidance and sample syllabus statement page.

methodsAttempted.query: UTK generative AI ethical evaluative use academic integrity privacy
methodsAttempted.notes: Found UTK Libraries guide with academic integrity, privacy, accuracy, and coursework guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official UT and UTK sources were discoverable without browser escalation. The UT System policy supplies systemwide requirements that include UT Knoxville. UTK Writing Center and UT Libraries pages provide campus guidance for student, instructor, attribution, academic integrity, and AI literacy claims. OIT and provost pages were retained as context-only; the College of Law page was scoped and not used for university-wide claims.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:utk.edu OR site:tennessee.edu University of Tennessee Knoxville generative AI artificial intelligence teaching policy academic integrity
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UT System policy expressly includes UT Knoxville in campus definition and applies systemwide to students, faculty, and staff for instructional and assignment purposes.

artifacts.sourceTitle: GenAI Tools and Writing: Information and Guidelines for Students
artifacts.queryUsed: UTK GenAI tools writing information guidelines students
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UTK Writing Center student-facing page gives concrete guidance on instructor permission, documentation, citation, and not replacing student writing with GenAI.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Sample syllabus statements for AI Guidelines
artifacts.queryUsed: UTK sample syllabus statements AI guidelines
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UTK Writing Center page describes Provost-provided suggestions and course-level open, moderate, and strict AI-use examples.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using Generative AI: Ethical & Evaluative Use
artifacts.queryUsed: UTK Libraries Generative AI ethical evaluative academic integrity privacy accuracy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UTK Libraries guide gives practical guidance on ethical/evaluative use, academic integrity, privacy, accuracy, attribution, and asking instructors.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence at UT: What You Need to Know
artifacts.queryUsed: UTK artificial intelligence what you need to know policy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official OIT news/advisory page points readers to the formal UT policy and resources, but it is too brief and context-oriented for a primary claim.

artifacts.queryUsed: UTK Provost AI in Higher Education
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Provost initiative page describes governance and committees, but does not itself provide narrow student or instructor AI-use rules.

artifacts.queryUsed: UTK College of Law AI policy
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official and substantive, but scoped to University of Tennessee College of Law. Not used for university-wide UT Knoxville claims in this bundle.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Everyone is responsible for understanding and following university policies and ethical guidelines when using AI.
artifacts.notes: Context-only OIT advisory; it links to BT0035, which was used as the primary policy source instead.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: This effort is designed to provide thoughtful and consistent campus leadership governing the use of generative AI tools across campus.
artifacts.notes: Governance and committee context only; not used for a specific policy claim.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The Law School has adopted the following policy: effective Spring 2025.
artifacts.notes: Scoped to the College of Law. It may support a future law-school-scoped claim, but this university-level bundle avoids overstating it.

targets.sourceTitle: GenAI Tools and Writing: Information and Guidelines for Students
Sample syllabus statements for AI Guidelines
Using Generative AI: Ethical & Evaluative Use

stopConditions: Stop when at least one formal UT policy source and one UTK campus guidance source have been fetched and evidence-linked.
Do not stage raw HTML, PDFs, screenshots, browser profiles, or logs in the run directory.
Do not use College of Law guidance for university-wide claims unless explicitly scoped.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Tennessee system AI policy, which includes UT Knoxville, expects course faculty or staff to communicate permitted AI uses for a course and says students are responsible for following those course-specific AI requirements.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Tennessee system AI policy says course-related communications must inform students that unpermitted AI use is a form of academic misconduct subject to the campus student code of conduct.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Tennessee system AI policy says protected university data must not be entered into AI technology that has not been reviewed by the University CIO or designee and authorized for use.

artifacts.claimText: UTK Writing Center guidance tells students that when an instructor allows GenAI for writing assignments, they should use it to assist rather than replace their writing process and should document and describe the use.

artifacts.notes: Claim is directly supported by BT0035 and scoped to UT System policy including UT Knoxville.
Claim uses the source's own academic misconduct wording and avoids broader sanctions claims.
Claim is limited to protected university data and authorized AI technology language in BT0035.
Claim is student-writing guidance, not a binding university-wide prohibition.
Claim is supported by two UTK Writing Center pages and scoped to course-level AI rules.
Claim uses UTK Libraries wording and does not present the page as a formal conduct code.

artifacts.title: The University of Tennessee, Knoxville AI Policy Staged Evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for The University of Tennessee, Knoxville includes the UT System BT0035 artificial intelligence policy and UTK campus guidance from the Writing Center and Libraries. Claims are limited to course communication expectations, unpermitted AI as academic misconduct, protected-data handling, student writing guidance, course-level variation, and academic-integrity guidance. Context-only OIT/provost pages and College of Law-scoped policy were rejected for university-wide claim use.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The College of Law AI policy was not used for university-wide claims because it is scoped to the law school.
The provost initiative page and OIT advisory were retained as official context but rejected as primary evidence because they did not provide sufficiently narrow policy language.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
